sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 45-year-old male, presented to the office with a 5-day history of right knee pain.
He reports that he was water skiing on Lake Martin and twisted his knee when he landed, resulting in pain and swelling.
He denies any numbness or tingling in his foot.
He has been taking over-the-counter Aleve twice daily and applying a cold pack to the affected area, which has provided some relief.
Past medical history: The patient had his tonsils removed.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: I prescribed Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure is 120/77.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is unremarkable, with no lymphadenopathy.
The heart exam is unremarkable, with a regular rate and rhythm, and no murmur.
The lung exam is clear to auscultation bilaterally.
The musculoskeletal exam reveals ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion on the right knee.
Palpation of the right medial knee is painful.
The patient has decreased range of motion, a negative varus and valgus test, a negative Lachman sign, and palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee, with inflammation and fluid accumulation.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain; hypertension; tetanus status: unknown.
Plan:
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any broken bones.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee to prevent further injury.
I prescribed Meloxicam 15mg once daily to reduce swelling and pain.
I continued the patient on Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
I ordered a lipid panel to ensure everything is okay from that standpoint.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: The patient will return for the results of the x-ray and to be discharged.
